name: Funderial Business Funding
description_short: Funderial Business Funding provides alternative business financing including term loans, merchant cash advances, equipment financing, and lines of credit to small and mid-market businesses with 6+ months operating history.
description_long:
Funderial Business Funding is an alternative business lending platform that has reportedly funded over $2 billion in business loans across more than 15 years of operation. The company positions itself as a financing aggregator, connecting business owners with a network of over 160 lending partners to access various financing products. Funderial claims to have served 20,000+ satisfied business owners seeking alternatives to traditional bank financing.

The company offers a diverse product suite including business term loans, merchant cash advances (MCAs), equipment financing, SBA loans, lines of credit, real estate financing, invoice factoring, MCA consolidation, and working capital loans. Funding amounts range from $3,500 to $5,000,000, with flexible terms spanning 3-6 months for short-term needs up to 10 years for long-term financing. The company claims businesses can receive funding within 24 hours to same-day turnaround in some cases, and advertises a simple qualification threshold: 6 months in business (or 3 months if accepting credit cards) and $10,000 monthly revenue from a business checking account.

Funderial distinguishes itself through several claimed features: free quotes requiring only a one-page application and 3 months of bank statements; a quote comparison system where multiple lenders compete for business; and ongoing financial consulting to help businesses access additional funding within 30-60 days of initial funding. The company emphasizes flexible, customized solutions rather than one-size-fits-all products, and positions itself as a long-term financial growth partner rather than a one-time lender.

While Funderial presents compelling marketing around speed and accessibility, potential borrowers should note that as a broker/marketplace rather than a direct lender, actual rates, terms, and approval depend on the partner lenders in their network. The company does not disclose APR ranges, specific lending criteria beyond the baseline requirements, or detailed information about partner lender terms. The emphasis on merchant cash advances—which typically carry high effective rates—suggests borrowers should carefully compare actual costs across funding types.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Claims same-day to 24-hour funding turnaround time compared to traditional bank timelines
Very low baseline requirements: only 6 months business history and $10,000 monthly revenue to qualify
Large funding range from $3,500 to $5,000,000 accommodating diverse business sizes
Free quote process requiring only 1-page application and 3 months of bank statements
Network of 160+ lending partners creates competitive quote comparison for rates and terms
Diverse product menu including SBA loans, term loans, lines of credit, and real estate financing
Ongoing financial consulting and repeat funding access claimed within 30-60 days of initial funding
cons: Operates as a marketplace/broker rather than direct lender, meaning actual rates and terms vary by partner lender and are not disclosed on website
Heavy marketing emphasis on merchant cash advances, which typically carry significantly higher effective interest rates than traditional loans
No specific APR ranges, fee structures, or concrete lending criteria disclosed—only marketing claims about speed and approval likelihood
Requires business checking account history and recent bank statements, excluding cash-based businesses and newer operations with less than 6 months history
No information provided about default rates, customer complaint history, or independent ratings from third parties
best_for: Business owners with 6+ months operating history unable to qualify for traditional bank loans due to credit or cash flow concerns
Established small businesses needing rapid capital access for expansion, equipment, or working capital within days rather than weeks
Companies seeking flexible alternatives willing to compare multiple lender offers to negotiate better rates and terms
Business owners open to various financing structures (term loans, MCAs, lines of credit) and willing to evaluate tradeoffs in rates vs. speed
diagnosis: Funderial is best for established small business owners who need rapid funding ($3,500-$5,000,000) and have acceptable business history/revenue but may not qualify for traditional bank loans. The primary caveat is that Funderial functions as a marketplace broker, not a direct lender—actual rates, terms, and approval depend entirely on the partner lenders in their 160+ network, and the emphasis on merchant cash advances means borrowers should carefully evaluate true costs, as MCAs typically carry much higher effective interest rates than traditional term loans.
services: Business Term Loans up to 250% of gross monthly revenue
Real Estate Financing for purchase, development, or refinancing
Free financial consulting and quote comparison services
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 65 W 36th St. Suite 901, New York, NY 10018
